title: Standard sanity-check ranges on vital signs and lab measurements
description: Apply standard sanity-check range constraints to named physiological measurement fields. The values below catch data-entry typos; out-of-range values almost always indicate a slip rather than a real outlier. Constraints emit as soft (warning-only) by default — coordinators can save and document the deviation. Hard-rejecting versions should only be used when explicitly required by the protocol; see OC-6 for the bind__oc_constraint_type='hard' opt-in mechanism.
rationale: Vital-sign and basic-lab ranges are well-established universal sanity bands. Catching typos at entry is far cheaper than catching them in source-data verification or post-hoc reconciliation. Soft constraints rather than hard ones preserve the coordinator's ability to capture genuinely unusual but real values with documentation.

set.field.constraint_message: Systolic BP out of expected range (60–250 mmHg). Verify entry.
Diastolic BP out of expected range (30–150 mmHg) or not less than systolic. Verify entry.
Pulse out of expected range (30–200 bpm). Verify entry.
Respiratory rate out of expected range (5–60 breaths/min). Verify entry.
Temperature out of expected range (34–42 °C). Verify entry.
Height out of expected adult range (100–250 cm). Verify entry.
Weight out of expected range (20–300 kg). Verify entry.
SpO2 out of expected range (0–100 %). Verify entry.
ECG heart rate out of expected range (30–200 bpm). Verify entry.

history.note: Imported from prompts.py RULE OC-7H during Phase B.1c-final. The first fully-structured OC-7 sub-rule. Uses match directive from B.1c-2 (commit 46ad938) with set directives in each case. The DIABP constraint references the sibling field ${SYSBP} — Claude needs to verify SYSBP exists on the same form when DIABP fires; if it doesn't, the constraint resolves to a missing-reference error at form-load time. A field.has_sibling guard could be added in B.1d to make this safer. Pediatric or geriatric studies need study-scope overrides on HEIGHT and WEIGHT ranges. Overlap candidate with B.0 inventory's §28 PRECISION_TABLE (which used a similar table-driven structure for decimal precision); both are now well-served by the match directive.
